findings.title: Hard-coded log path diverges from the existing log configuration model
findings.impact: The new command bakes in `/opt/yuanrong/datasystem/log` and the `<address>.log` naming convention, while the existing worker config and logging code use configurable `log_dir` and `log_filename` values such as `datasystem_worker.INFO.log`. Future changes to the worker logging layout will have to remember this separate convention, and readers cannot tell which source of truth owns log discovery.
findings.fix: Derive the log directory and filename from the same worker configuration/process flags used by `start` and the logging subsystem, or centralize a helper that documents the supported log-file naming contract instead of duplicating a hard-coded path.

findings.title: Discovery comments point to a non-existent status command
findings.impact: The comment says this mirrors `stop`/`status`, but the current CLI modules and registration list do not contain a `status` command. Future maintainers following the comment will search for a non-existent implementation and may misunderstand which discovery behavior is actually being reused.
findings.fix: Update the code comment and matching design text to reference only the existing `stop.py` behavior, or add the shared status/discovery helper if that command is intended to exist.
